identity.role: kappa = ln(phi)/16 conservation law and conserved morphon potential

pattern: complete-unformalized work -> formalization in paper-bound space -> Forge
note_on_pattern: Here the Forge already existed (ChromaForge.conservation held the kappa ledger) but was bound only to a stale memory-DB ref. This promotion supplies the missing half: the repo-paper formalization (verifier + receipt in formal-papers/CQE-paper-09) plus the morphon coupling/embedding module, and rebinds the registry entry to the live repo paper.

source_work.state_before: conservation law live across services (conservation.py ledger, engine.py morphon embedding, portal/glyph/atlas constants) but never bound to a repo proof paper; ChromaForge carried the ledger with paper_ref pointing at a stale memory-DB markdown

adjudicated_divergence: CMPLX-TMN-main contains an internal sign contradiction: src/conservation/conservation.py flags delta_phi > 0 as a violation and /surplus treats cumulative_dphi < 0 as spendable (conserved = negative), while src/engine/engine.py emits a strictly POSITIVE morphon_delta and comments it as 'conserved surplus' — backwards. The live ChromaForge/PaneForge Event Law receipt mints delta_phi = -kappa (negative), confirming the conservation.py convention. ChromaForge.morphon emits the conserved negative delta.
FastAPI/psycopg2/PG-backed ledger service layers stay product-side; the forge carries the stdlib conservation core
validated_claims: kappa = ln(phi)/16 numerically (= 0.030075739066...), matching the source constant 0.030076.
Golden-ratio identities phi^2 = phi+1 and e^(16 kappa) = phi.
The E8 embedding sits on the kappa-sphere: norm exactly kappa, deterministic from content hash.
The morphon potential delta is conserved (<= 0) and bounded by kappa*affinity.
The sector split delta_n + delta_i + delta_l = delta_phi (Noether / Shannon / Landauer).
The Event Law per-event emission is exactly -kappa, the value minted in every live ChromaForge/PaneForge receipt (cross-repo confirmation).
A stream of conserved deltas keeps the cumulative non-increasing with zero violations (Hamiltonian descent).
A single positive delta is flagged as a violation; only positive deltas are.
The audit chain recomputes the cumulative with zero drift.
Surplus is the spendable magnitude when the cumulative is negative.
honesty_boundary: The three-sector split is modeled as equal thirds; deriving unequal Noether/Shannon/Landauer weights from content is not claimed. The 'law IS the stop condition IS palindromic closure' equivalence asserted in the source prose is not yet given its own receipt.
open_obligations: Patch CMPLX-TMN-main engine.py morphon_delta sign upstream to match the conservation service and the live receipt.
Receipt for the palindromic-closure equivalence claim.
Content-derived sector weights instead of equal thirds.
Next profile-repo candidates: CMPLXMCP AGRM controller (auto-escalate inline->MDHG), CMPLXDevKit MDHG golden-ratio sweep cache, chart8 D4 atlas bijectivity (ConvergeForge obligation).
notes: Eighth promotion of the loop; third from the profile-repo lane. Ties the geometric-computer conservation law of CMPLX-TMN-main to the Event Law already running in the kernel: kappa = ln(phi)/16 is simultaneously TMN's universal coupling and the magnitude of the per-event delta_phi the kernel mints. The sign adjudication resolves which of TMN's two contradictory conventions the corpus actually uses.
